title: Chief Of Hearts

jokes.text: I don't remember if Dylan's a boy.
jokes.setup: Going to a child's birthday party for someone they supposedly know
jokes.punchline: Homer admits he doesn't even know the child's gender
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness with good economy - reveals character while creating awkward situation

jokes.text: Of course you do. Dylan's a... Let me check the invitation.
jokes.setup: Marge confidently asserts Homer knows Dylan
jokes.punchline: Mid-sentence realizes she doesn't know either
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the setup - shows both parents are clueless despite Marge's initial confidence

jokes.text: Fish theme? That tells me nothing!
jokes.setup: Looking for gender clues on party invitation
jokes.punchline: Fish theme is completely unhelpful for determining gender
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about the uselessness of generic party themes

jokes.text: See? Dylan's clearly a gir...
jokes.setup: Marge finds what she thinks is conclusive evidence
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off mid-word, implying the evidence contradicts her
jokes.explanation: Clever use of interruption for misdirection - the visual must contradict what she was about to say

jokes.text: What kind of party has two activities? They're just trying to confuse me.
jokes.setup: Finding activities that don't clearly indicate gender
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks it's a deliberate conspiracy against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer paranoia - turning innocent party planning into personal persecution

jokes.text: I give up! I hope Dylan likes trucks.
jokes.setup: Struggling to determine child's gender for appropriate gift
jokes.punchline: Gives up and defaults to stereotypically masculine choice
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer solution - when in doubt, go with assumptions and hope for the best

jokes.text: Lady, you don't know Dylan!
jokes.setup: Homer's assumption about Dylan liking trucks
jokes.punchline: Child calls out that they don't actually know Dylan
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the whole sequence - child cuts through the adult confusion with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Carrot cake! So moist!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering carrot cake at party
jokes.punchline: Over-enthusiastic reaction to cake moistness
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer food obsession, somewhat predictable but in character

jokes.text: It's a Japanese card game based on a cartoon based on an ancient religion based on a candy bar.
jokes.setup: Explaining the complexity of Battle Balls card game
jokes.punchline: Absurd chain of adaptation sources ending with candy bar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of media adaptation chains with perfect escalating absurdity. High craft for its economy and originality.

jokes.text: It makes Digimon look like Pokemon.
jokes.setup: Describing Battle Balls complexity
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical comparison between similar franchises
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up joke playing on the meaninglessness of comparing nearly identical franchises

jokes.text: Better lie low, my sweet red friend.
jokes.setup: Sign says 'No food allowed in bank'
jokes.punchline: Homer treating candy apple like contraband that needs hiding
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's relationship with food and rule-breaking paranoia

jokes.text: And he wants $1 million in unmarked bills! That would be nice.
jokes.setup: News report about bank robbery demands
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual agreement that a million dollars would be nice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - missing the point of the news story to focus on the money aspect

jokes.text: It's just a candy apple.
jokes.setup: Police treating Homer as armed robber
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'weapon' is just his candy apple from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier candy apple setup, pays off the misdirection well

jokes.text: Community service? But... that's work. What about jail?
jokes.setup: Judge sentences Homer to community service
jokes.punchline: Homer prefers jail because community service is work
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - preferring incarceration to actual work shows his priorities perfectly

jokes.text: Free food. Teardrop tattoos. Library books that come to you. I'll serve anything but the community.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he wants jail
jokes.punchline: Lists prison 'benefits' ending with hatred of serving community
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect Homer selfishness - the final line caps it beautifully

jokes.text: Man, that thing sure meets a lot. You know, I've read that book. Maybe I could come and... Right, not enough chairs.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to join Marge's book club
jokes.punchline: Marge's transparent excuse about chair shortage
jokes.explanation: Good marital awkwardness - Marge's weak excuse reveals she doesn't want Homer there

jokes.text: Chicken parm, meatball parm, eggplant parm, shish ka-parm, angel food parm, moo goo gai parm. My wife can parm anything.
jokes.setup: Homer listing his lunch contents
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd items all made into parmesan dishes
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation with 'parm' becoming a verb, shows Marge's cooking obsession through Homer's pride

jokes.text: My wife only parms on my birthday. And that's only if I give her the sad puppy dog look.
jokes.setup: Homer bragging about Marge's parmesan skills
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's wife requires begging for basic cooking
jokes.explanation: Good contrast to Homer's situation, establishes Wiggum's sad home life

jokes.text: Krustofsky, go sort those bums by the war they were in.
jokes.setup: Assigning community service tasks
jokes.punchline: Sorting homeless people by which war they served in
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever commentary on veteran homelessness with absurdist organizational twist

jokes.text: Throw a jean jacket on top of these babies, and you can go anywhere. From fast food to fine dining?
jokes.setup: Discussing the versatility of police pants
jokes.punchline: Jean jacket makes police pants appropriate for all dining
jokes.explanation: Good bonding moment with mild fashion absurdity, builds their friendship

jokes.text: They call me 'Daddy Round-Round.' Send me a Christmas card every year.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his custom underwear from Ukraine
jokes.punchline: Ukrainian village's affectionate nickname and ongoing relationship
jokes.explanation: Creative and character-specific - the Christmas card detail makes it both sweet and absurd

jokes.text: My underpants paid for their water filtration system.
jokes.setup: Homer's relationship with Ukrainian underwear makers
jokes.punchline: His underwear needs funded village infrastructure
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the underwear joke with unexpected global impact implications

jokes.text: Earth to Homer, I arrested you there last month.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Wiggum has been to Moe's
jokes.punchline: Wiggum arrested Homer at that exact location recently
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their history and Homer's obliviousness to past encounters

jokes.text: Who could blame you? Your sports team lost.
jokes.setup: Wiggum apologizing for being a jerk during arrest
jokes.punchline: Homer reduces police professionalism to sports disappointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - reducing complex police behavior to simple sports fandom

jokes.text: He's not my friend. He's just a non violent perp I'm gonna pound a few beers with.
jokes.setup: Other cops noting Wiggum made a friend
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's denial using cop terminology while describing friendship activity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's professional facade cracking over personal connection

jokes.text: So this is what it feels like to be a pregnant lady.
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing police escort through traffic
jokes.punchline: Compares VIP treatment to pregnancy privileges
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor connecting police privilege to pregnancy perks

jokes.text: So this is what it feels like to be a pregnant lady.
jokes.setup: Homer riding in police car with sirens
jokes.punchline: Compares VIP treatment to pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Homer's odd comparison shows his unique perspective on privilege.

jokes.text: My not-dead grandma sent it from Tokyo.
jokes.setup: Ralph explaining how he got a rare card
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specifies grandmother is 'not-dead'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum logic - unnecessary clarification that creates unintended dark humor

jokes.text: I see you're impressed by my costume to the school's annual drug-awareness assembly.
jokes.setup: Other characters reacting to Wiggum's appearance
jokes.punchline: Reveals the suspicious-looking setup is just anti-drug costume
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection playing on drug bust expectations vs. innocent education

jokes.text: Drunk and disorderly? That's a little redundant.
jokes.setup: Homer reading his police record
jokes.punchline: Points out the logical overlap in the charges
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about legal terminology redundancy

jokes.text: You put yourself on AMBER Alert.
jokes.setup: Homer playing with police computer system
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow listed himself as a missing child
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - Homer's computer incompetence creates impossible scenario

jokes.text: Cops don't have many friends. Civilians are scared of us, and other cops remind us of things we want to forget.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining why their friendship is special
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly deep insight into police isolation
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly profound character moment that gives depth to usually comic figure

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, jailbird. Now I've got you on burglary and killing a moment.
jokes.setup: Prisoner interrupting emotional conversation
jokes.punchline: Wiggum charges him with 'killing a moment'
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay turning emotional disruption into criminal charge

jokes.text: Bart has always been what we educators term a no-goodnik.
jokes.setup: Formal educational assessment
jokes.punchline: Using silly made-up term 'no-goodnik' as professional jargon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner pseudo-intellectualism. 'No-goodnik' sounds official but isn't.

jokes.text: Dealing drugs? That's impossible. He doesn't have the math skills.
jokes.setup: Principal suggesting Bart is dealing drugs
jokes.punchline: Marge's concern is about math ability, not morality
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - focuses on practical obstacle rather than moral objection, very Marge

jokes.text: What if the bed wets him? What if Superman decided to kill everybody?
jokes.setup: Discussing Ralph's worries
jokes.punchline: Absurd fears including bed wetting him and evil Superman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph-style concerns with escalation from mundane to cosmic fears

jokes.text: You need backup, or you want backup?
jokes.setup: Lou calling for backup during shootout
jokes.punchline: Wiggum questioning the semantics during emergency
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence - focusing on word choice during crisis

jokes.text: Who are you, the rules police? We are the police police.
jokes.setup: Lou objecting to Homer helping with arrest
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'rules police' vs actual police
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay moment with nice escalation from metaphorical to literal police

jokes.text: Take your hands off my brother from another mother.
jokes.setup: Homer defending Wiggum from criminals
jokes.punchline: Using street slang to describe their friendship
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer's loyalty with mild slang humor

jokes.text: His left lung was filled with shamrock shake. McDonald's didn't even sell them, this year.
jokes.setup: Explaining Wiggum's medical condition
jokes.punchline: Impossible injury made worse by seasonal menu information
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist medical diagnosis with perfect unnecessary detail about McDonald's menu

jokes.text: You're too late. I've been sleeping on that couch for a week. You missed the wakeup. That's all that counts.
jokes.setup: Lou arriving after Wiggum wakes up
jokes.punchline: Lou's dedication shown through week of couch sleeping, dismissing timing
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Lou's actual dedication vs. surface appearance

jokes.text: It should be about an imaginary meeting between Babe Ruth and Hitler. But make no mention of baseball or Germany. And... begin.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking Homer to tell him a story
jokes.punchline: Impossible creative constraints for the story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist request - creates impossible storytelling challenge that's both specific and contradictory

jokes.text: Not until you raise my allowance.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Bart is on drugs
jokes.punchline: Bart treats drugs like negotiable commodity tied to allowance
jokes.explanation: Good Bart response turning serious question into business negotiation

jokes.text: It's no fun if my Mom likes 'em.
jokes.setup: Marge approving of Bart's card game
jokes.punchline: Bart loses interest when parents approve
jokes.explanation: Perfect child psychology - rebellion loses appeal when parents approve

jokes.text: People who get shot in the chest are such big babies.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his absence to family
jokes.punchline: Dismisses gunshot wounds as minor complaints
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer insensitivity - treating serious injury as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Maybe I lied, but you've been so needy. 'I'm hungry','I'm itchy', 'That's not my blood type'.
jokes.setup: Homer justifying lying to Wiggum about event
jokes.punchline: Lists complaints escalating to medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from minor complaints to life-threatening medical issue treated equally

jokes.text: If it was, he'd be getting the chair. You all would! Bad friend!
jokes.setup: Arresting Homer for being a bad friend
jokes.punchline: Escalates punishment and indicts all police officers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Wiggum's hurt feelings through legal fantasy

jokes.text: You both jive. You hear me? You both jive.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony being arrested
jokes.punchline: Uses outdated slang as serious insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Fat Tony using dated slang as ultimate condemnation

jokes.text: Looks like his weapon's loaded with 12 original herbs and spices.
jokes.setup: Police surrounding Fat Tony with food
jokes.punchline: KFC reference for food 'weapon'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun relying on brand reference without much creativity or setup

jokes.text: No. Faith is what you have in things that don't exist. Your awesomeness is real.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking if Homer has faith in him
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpectedly philosophical distinction
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly deep Homer moment with clever wordplay about faith vs. evidence

jokes.text: Hot cocoa? No, ice cold.
jokes.setup: Flanders asking about hot cocoa
jokes.punchline: Correction to make it less pleasant
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist moment making prank instruction more unpleasant

tmdb_overview: Homer is completing his court-ordered community service when he befriends his supervisor, Chief Wiggum, by offering him one of his sandwiches. Touched by the act of kindness, Wiggum assigns the other convicts unpleasant tasks, but allows Homer to join him at the picnic table. They continue to grow close, but when the Chief gets injured during a botched bank robbery, Homer doesn't come through when Wiggum needs him the most. Meanwhile, Bart becomes addicted to Battle Ball, a Japanese game made up of plastic balls and magnetic cards, and his family and teachers try to help him kick the habit.
